candidate.description: Hash-ring-based partitioning that minimizes key remapping when nodes are added or removed; commonly used for sharded caches and distributed databases.

candidates_full.full_description: Hash-based key-to-node mapping arranged on a ring such that adding or removing a node only remaps keys near the affected segment, minimizing data movement during cluster topology changes. Commonly extended with virtual nodes for load balance.
Partitioning data by contiguous key ranges; supports range scans but is prone to hot spots.
